Definition.text: Curse is a category of Debuff split into Hexes and Marks.

Purpose.text: Apply negative modifiers to enemies (or allies in specific cases) through skill-driven debuffing.

Core Rules.text: Each curse has default values; applying by skill replaces those defaults. Curses from the player are removed when the cursing source is lost (death, area leave, losing the skill).

Internal Rules.text: Hexes can apply on multiple targets; Marks are single-target curse state with no duration and replacement behavior.

Calculation Order.text: 1) curse skill resolves 2) curse limit check for that source context 3) remove oldest curse if required 4) apply new curse state 5) start curse-specific duration/state handling.

Priority Rules.text: By default one curse can be active on a creature; additional curses depend on curse limit and are constrained by Blasphemy/curse priority rules.

Preconditions.text: A valid curse source and applicable target are required; targets with Hexproof or curse immunity are unaffected by matching source logic.

Postconditions.text: Target enters cursed state according to curse type constraints and remains until expiration, loss of source, or replacement.

Limitations.text: Curse limit can be zero, in which case curse application does not apply a curse state. Monsters and maps may enforce Hexproof/Immunity.

Caps.text: Default curse slot is one per target; additional slots come from mechanics that increase curse limit.

Formula.text: No single universal curse formula is shared across all curse types; each curse applies its own debuff rule set and scaling by Curse Effect sources.

Conflicts.text: Blasphemy curse auras and self-cast curse skills can conflict for application priority.

Exceptions.text: Map curses do not count against player curse limit and cannot be permanently removed by normal dispel pathways.

Hidden Rules.text: Some curse text can appear in non-skill forms (for example monster auras and map mods) that do not use the same duration/removal path as skill casts.

Developer Notes.text: Do not conflate curse category with damage-dealer mechanics; curse resolution includes independent removal, priority, and slot constraints.

Historical Changes.text: Curse system behavior around removal and source loss was reworked with 3.12-era changes in totems/monster curse immunity handling.

text.note: Curse immunity removals and totem-cursing bug fixes were introduced, and some additional monster curse immunity entries were removed.
Some totems could now be cursed and additional totem curse handling fixes were documented.

text: Applying a second curse can expire the oldest curse on the same target when limit is reached.
A Mark from one source replaces the previous Mark from that source family.
Curse is not equivalent to Debuff in all cases.
Map curses and skill-cast curses do not always use identical remove/limit behavior.

prevents: infinite curse stacking without additional slots
overrides: oldest curse on target when limit is full
